originalTitle: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
title: President Biya Gets a Helping Hand!
content:
Hello, hello, wonderful friends! I'm your friendly news reporter right here from sunny Africa! Today, we have some super interesting news from a country called Cameroon. It's a beautiful place with lots of green trees and amazing animals.

Meet President Paul Biya!

In Cameroon, there is a very important man. He is the leader of the whole country! His name is President Paul Biya. He is a very, very old man. Can you guess how old he is? He is 93 years old! That's older than your grandparents, and maybe even older than your great-grandparents! Did you know? Being 93 years old means he has seen so many sunrises and sunsets! He has lived through many, many years. It's like having 93 birthday parties!

A Long, Long Time as President

President Biya has been the leader of Cameroon for a very, very long time. Imagine you are 7 or 8 years old. Now imagine being president for 43 years! That's how long he has been in charge. He started being president when your parents were just little babies, or maybe not even born yet! Did you know? 43 years is longer than most people go to school and then work! It's like being in charge for nearly half a century. A century is 100 years!

What Does a President Do?

A president is like the head of a very big family, but it's the country! A president helps make sure things like schools, roads, and hospitals are good for everyone. They help keep people safe and happy. It's a very big job with lots of important decisions to make every day. Imagine being in charge of your whole classroom. That's a big job, right? Now imagine being in charge of a whole country with millions of people! That's what a president does.

A New Helper is Coming!

Now, here is the really special news! After being the president all by himself for 43 years, President Biya is getting a helper! This helper is called a "deputy." You can think of a deputy as a very important assistant. Imagine you are building a big, big sandcastle. It's hard to do all by yourself, right? But if your friend helps you, it becomes easier and more fun! That's what a deputy does for the president.

Why is a Deputy Important?

Having a helper is a very good thing. The deputy will help President Biya with all his important tasks. They can share the work. This means the president will have someone to talk to about big ideas and someone to help him get things done. It's like having two hands instead of one for a big job. Or like having a friend help you carry heavy books. Teamwork makes everything better! It means the country can run even more smoothly.

First Time in 43 Years!

This is why the news is extra special! President Biya has not had a main helper like this for 43 whole years. He has been doing this super big job largely on his own for a very, very long time. Now, he will have someone new right by his side. Think about how much has changed in 43 years! We have new phones, new cars, and new ways to learn. And now, Cameroon will have a new way of leadership with a deputy.

Where is Cameroon?

Cameroon is a beautiful country in West Africa. It's famous for its diverse landscapes, which means it has lots of different kinds of nature! It has sandy beaches, tall mountains, and big, green rainforests. Many amazing animals live there. Did you know? Cameroon is sometimes called "Africa in miniature" because it has so many different kinds of places and people, just like the whole continent of Africa! It has elephants, gorillas, and many colorful birds.

Working Together for Cameroon

This new deputy will work very closely with President Biya. They will be a team! Good teams always help each other. When leaders work together, they can make even better plans for their country. It's like playing a football game. You need a whole team to win, not just one player! Everyone has a role to play. The president and his new deputy will be a team for Cameroon.

Did You Know? Fun Facts!

Did you know that the word "deputy" means someone who is chosen to act for or help another person? It's like a helper who can also lead sometimes. Did you know that some countries have a president and a vice-president? A vice-president is a kind of deputy! Did you know that teamwork is important in every part of life, from playing games to leading a country?

What This Means for Cameroon

With a new deputy, the president will have more support. This can help the country in many ways. It means more ideas can be thought of and more projects can be started. It's like adding more helpers to build a giant puzzle. The more helpers, the faster and better the puzzle gets done! Everyone hopes that this new step will bring even more good things for all the people of Cameroon. It's a fresh start in some ways, bringing new energy to a very important job.

A Bright Future Together

So, that's the big news from Cameroon! President Paul Biya, who is 93 years old and has been president for 43 years, is getting a new deputy. This special helper will work with him to lead the country. It shows that even the oldest and most experienced leaders need a helping hand sometimes. We wish President Biya and his new deputy all the best as they work together for the wonderful people of Cameroon. It's a reminder that working as a team is always the best way forward!
summary: President Paul Biya of Cameroon, who is 93 years old and has been a leader for 43 years, will soon have a special helper called a deputy for the very first time.
whyMatters: This is important because it means the very old president will get help with his big job, and working as a team can make things better for all the people in Cameroon.
